name: HIPAA Safeguards (US health data)
description: Deterministic guards for codebases touching PHI: SSN literals, PHI in log statements, PHI in URLs/query strings, plaintext transport. Supports §164.312 technical-safeguard evidence — evidence, not certification.

policies.name: no-ssn-literals
policies.rule: Social Security Numbers must never appear as literals in source code, fixtures, or comments.
policies.skill: Use obviously-fake placeholders (000-00-0000 fails validation everywhere) or generated fixtures. Real SSNs in code are a reportable disclosure.
policies.fixSuggestion: Remove the SSN and use a generated or clearly-invalid placeholder.

policies.name: no-phi-in-logs
policies.rule: Patient identifiers (SSN, MRN, DOB, diagnosis) must not be written to logs.
policies.skill: Log opaque record IDs, never the PHI fields themselves. If debugging needs patient context, log the internal UUID and look the record up in the system of record.
policies.fixSuggestion: Log the internal record UUID instead of patient-identifying fields.

policies.name: no-phi-in-urls
policies.rule: PHI must not be passed in URLs or query strings — URLs land in access logs, browser history, and proxies.
policies.skill: Send PHI in the request body over HTTPS. Use opaque identifiers in paths (/patients/{uuid}), never ?ssn= or ?dob=.
policies.fixSuggestion: Move the identifier to the request body or use an opaque UUID path parameter.

policies.name: require-https-endpoints
policies.rule: Service endpoints must use HTTPS — PHI in transit must be encrypted.
policies.skill: Default every endpoint constant and config to https://. http:// is acceptable only for localhost development.
policies.fixSuggestion: Change the endpoint to https:// (http is only acceptable for localhost).
